Off Calendar Sep-02-2022 Continued To Nov-28-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For (1) Additional Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel Fulvio F. Cajina, Jointly And Severally, In The Amount Of $5,710 For Failure To Follow Court Order And Failure To Provide Discovery Responses, And (2) To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Aug-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT POWER ELECTRONICS' Motion For (1) Additional Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel Fulvio F. Cajina, Jointly And Severally, In The Amount Of $5,710 For Failure To Follow Court Order And Failure To Provide Discovery Responses, And (2) To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's unopposed Motion to Compel responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One and Request for Production of Documents, Set One is GRANTED. Plaintiff Melba Cajina is ORDERED to serve complete, verified responses to Defendant's Special Interrogatories Set One, and Request for Production of Documents, Sets One within 10 days of Notice of Entry of the Order on this motion. The Court notes and cautions Plaintiff that failure to provide responses to discovery in violation of the Court Order entered on the Register of Actions on April 18, 2022 is in violation of Code of Civil Procedure Sec. 2031.300, subdivision (c) providing in pertinent part that, if a party fails to obey an order compelling initial responses to requests for production of documents, the court may make those orders that are just, including the imposition of an issue sanction, an evidence sanction, or a terminating sanction. In lieu of, or in addition to, (non-monetary sanctions) the court may impose a monetary sanction" (Code of Civ. Proc., Sec. 2031.300 (c).) Plaintiff is ORDERED to pay sanctions to Defendant in the amount of $1,000.00 which shall accompany the discovery responses.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at peter.vanzandt@lewisbrisbois.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
